data.narrative:
Space Forge, a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $22.6M to date, is valued here against a comparable universe of 5 public firms (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression uses the $22.6M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $45M. Peer-Median anchors to a set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables (ClearSpace at $400M Series A; Aalyria at $1.3B Series B; Hubble Network and Sift both at $400M and $274M respectively in Series B), producing a median valuation of $400M with a range of $274M–$1.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied: while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 59.8x price-to-sales across six tickers, Space Forge's revenue is not on file, precluding revenue-multiple application.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $38M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $223M. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by the Peer-Median method's $400M result against Stage-Progression's more conservative $45M—reflects substantive uncertainty around whether Space Forge's position and trajectory align with mid-stage private peers or track earlier-stage funding multiples. Revenue visibility and clarification of product-market traction would materially narrow this band.
